setup_requirements: J-hooks at mid-chest, safety pins just below bottom depth.
setup_instructions: Set feet shoulder width, grip bar evenly, create upper-back shelf, and unrack with two controlled steps.
execution_steps: Inhale and brace before descent.
Sit down and slightly back while tracking knees over toes.
Reach at least parallel hip crease depth.
Drive through midfoot and stand without hips shooting up first.
breathing_pattern: Inhale and brace before each rep; exhale near lockout.
bracing_cues: Ribs down, abs tight 360°, squeeze bar into your back.
range_of_motion_target: Hip crease at or below top of knee with neutral spine.
safety_notes: Use safeties; stop set when depth or spinal position is lost.
spotting_notes_if_needed: Spotter on each side or use rack safeties for heavy attempts.
who_this_is_best_for: Lifters building total lower-body strength and size.
injury_considerations: Modify depth for hip impingement or knee pain; use pain-free ROM.
mobility_requirements: Ankle dorsiflexion and hip external rotation to hit depth.
plain_language_summary: This exercise trains your legs and hips by sitting down with a bar on your back and standing up strong.
feel_cue: You should feel quads and glutes working hard out of the bottom.
self_check_cue: If heels lift or knees cave, lower the weight and slow the descent.

setup_requirements: Eyes under bar, feet planted, shoulder blades pinned to bench.
setup_instructions: Set five points of contact: head, upper back, glutes, and both feet.
execution_steps: Unrack with straight wrists and stacked elbows.
Lower bar to lower chest with forearms vertical.
Briefly control touch, then press up and back to lockout.
Keep shoulder blades retracted throughout.
breathing_pattern: Inhale before lowering; exhale through sticking point.
bracing_cues: Brace abs and drive feet into floor without lifting glutes.
range_of_motion_target: Bar touches lower chest with elbows ~45-75° from torso.
safety_notes: Use collars and a competent spotter for near-failure work.
spotting_notes_if_needed: Hand-off and spot recommended above ~85% 1RM.
who_this_is_best_for: Beginner to advanced lifters needing upper-body press strength.
injury_considerations: Shoulder irritation may improve with neutral-grip dumbbell alternative.
mobility_requirements: Thoracic extension and shoulder external rotation tolerance.
primary_joint_action: Horizontal shoulder adduction and elbow extension
plain_language_summary: This exercise builds chest, shoulder, and triceps strength by pressing a bar away from your chest.
feel_cue: You should feel chest and triceps driving the bar off your chest.
self_check_cue: If your butt lifts off the bench, reduce load and re-brace.

setup_requirements: Bench at 15-30° incline.
setup_instructions: Prepare for Incline Dumbbell Press with controlled setup and stable alignment.
execution_steps: Start in the prescribed setup for Incline Dumbbell Press.
Execute the working range with control and target-muscle focus.
Hold the hardest position briefly.
Return to start without losing posture.
breathing_pattern: Inhale on the easier phase and exhale through effort.
bracing_cues: Brace trunk before each rep and keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
range_of_motion_target: Use the largest pain-free range while maintaining joint alignment.
safety_notes: Choose loads that preserve form; stop before technical breakdown.
spotting_notes_if_needed: Spotter optional unless near-failure with free weights.
who_this_is_best_for: Lifters who need a scalable option for this movement pattern.
injury_considerations: Use pain-free ROM and adjust grips or stance if joint irritation appears.
mobility_requirements: Basic joint mobility for the pattern and control at end ranges.
plain_language_summary: This exercise trains your chest using a controlled push horizontal pattern.
feel_cue: You should feel it mostly in your chest with support from front delts, triceps.
self_check_cue: If you feel joints more than muscles, reduce load and tighten your setup.

Hold the hardest position briefly.
Return to start without losing posture.
breathing_pattern: Inhale on the easier phase and exhale through effort.
bracing_cues: Brace trunk before each rep and keep ribs stacked over pelvis.
range_of_motion_target: Use the largest pain-free range while maintaining joint alignment.
safety_notes: Choose loads that preserve form; stop before technical breakdown.
spotting_notes_if_needed: Spotter optional unless near-failure with free weights.
who_this_is_best_for: Lifters who need a scalable option for this movement pattern.
injury_considerations: Use pain-free ROM and adjust grips or stance if joint irritation appears.
mobility_requirements: Basic joint mobility for the pattern and control at end ranges.
plain_language_summary: This exercise trains your full body using a controlled hinge pattern.
feel_cue: You should feel it mostly in your full body with support from quads, glutes.
self_check_cue: If you feel joints more than muscles, reduce load and tighten your setup.
